title: 1.1: Reports the target state as Normal and reason code as MissingSeries for stale series
1.2: Explains that a series becomes stale after missing two consecutive evaluation intervals, distinguishing genuinely stopped series from temporarily slow ones.
1.3: Identifies the staleness boundary formula showing that series become stale when lastEval + 2*interval <= evaluatedAt (e.g., stale=false at 1 interval, stale=true at 2 intervals for a 10s rule)

1.4: Explains the NeedsSending evaluation showing how resend delay, resolved retention, and last sent timestamp interact to control notification flow, including the ResolvedRetention duration (e.g., 15 minutes default) that terminates resolution notifications and the comparison logic (LastEvaluationTime.Sub(*ResolvedAt) > resolvedRetention)
1.5: States that screenshot capture (takeImage) is only triggered for stale series that were previously Alerting (e.g., called for Alerting, not called for Pending/Normal/NoData/Error)
1.6 States that stale series bypass the For duration requirement (e.g., Pending series transitions immediately to Normal without waiting)
1.7: Reports that stale series are deleted from cache when they vanish (e.g., cache lookup returns nil after staleness detection, deleteRuleStates removes the entry)
1.8: Reports that reappearing series with identical labels create fresh State instances with no memory of previous incarnation (e.g., new StartsAt timestamp, nil LastSentAt)
